description: This file defines possessive constructions and vowel contraction rules in Njo Viri (Balanda). Balanda distinguishes between intrinsic possession (body parts, name) and non-intrinsic possession (objects, external belongings). Contraction occurs when vowels meet at word boundaries.

general_principle.statement: Balanda marks possession differently depending on whether the noun is intrinsically possessed (body parts, name) or externally possessed (objects, belongings). Vowel contraction applies when adjacent vowels meet.

possessive_formation.principle: Personal pronouns do NOT mark possession by themselves.

intrinsic_possession.definition: Used for naturally or inherently possessed items such as body parts and name.
constraints: Do NOT use 'ngandi' for intrinsic possession.
Apply vowel contraction rules when vowels meet.

orthography_rules: Merged vowels are written as e'e or o'o.
Deleted vowels are marked with an apostrophe before ŋ.
Contraction is mandatory when vowel collision occurs.
error_constraints: Do not treat pronouns as possessive adjectives.
Do not use 'ngandi' with intrinsic possession.
Do not omit 'ngandi' with external possessions.
Do not ignore vowel contraction rules.
Do not leave vowel collisions unresolved.
